authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
Sacramento Municipal Utility District (Upper American River Project, FERC No. 2101)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Junction Reservoir not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Junction Reservoir not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (no entry for Junction Reservoir; El Dorado Co. entries cover Icehouse Reservoir and Union Valley Reservoir only)
GNIS Feature Detail Report (mirror) — Junction Reservoir, El Dorado County, CA (Feature ID 275023)
Reservoir Conditions: Junction Reservoir — SMUD Upper American River Project
sources.quotedText: Junction Reservoir is located at the junction of two canyons. Access is downstream from Union Valley Dam on a spur road off Bryant Springs Road (Forest Road 31). Boat launch/ramp elevation: 4,435 feet.

sources.title: Hydropower Project Summary — Upper American River Project, California (FERC No. 2101)
sources.quotedText: Junction Reservoir lies directly downstream of Union Valley Reservoir, and tailrace water from Union Valley Powerhouse flows into Junction Reservoir. Water is released from Junction Reservoir into Silver Creek, which runs 8.3 miles before entering Camino Reservoir.

sources.title: Junction Reservoir fishing reports — Fishbrain
sources.quotedText: Brown trout, Rainbow trout, and Steelhead

parameters.note: Junction Reservoir is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Junction Reservoir is a SMUD (Sacramento Municipal Utility District) hydroelectric storage reservoir on South Fork Silver Creek below Icehouse Dam, part of the Upper American River Project (FERC No. 2101). It receives tailrace flow from the Union Valley Powerhouse and releases into Silver Creek toward Camino Reservoir. Public access is via a spur road off Bryant Springs Road (Forest Road 31) to a boat launch at approximately 4,435 feet elevation, within Eldorado National Forest's Crystal Basin Recreation Area.

regulations.citation: SMUD Upper American River Project — Junction Reservoir recreation/reservoir-conditions page; FERC P-2101 Project Summary
regulations.humanSummary: Small SMUD hydro reservoir on South Fork Silver Creek below Icehouse Dam (Upper American River Project); accessed via a boat launch off Bryant Springs Road in Eldorado National Forest's Crystal Basin Recreation Area.
regulations.verbatimText: Junction Reservoir is located at the junction of two canyons. Access is downstream from Union Valley Dam on a spur road off Bryant Springs Road (Forest Road 31). Boat launch/ramp elevation: 4,435 feet.
